objective: Create internal/git/plumbing.go (NEW file in the existing `package git`) exposing the four snapshot-based atomic-commit plumbing primitives consumed by the generate commit step (P1.M6.T1.S1) and asserted by the safety-invariant tests (P1.M6.T3.S3): `RevParseHEAD() (sha string, hasParent bool, err error)` (`git rev-parse HEAD`; an unborn/rootless repo returns hasParent=false with a NIL error, never a failure), `WriteTree() (sha, err)` (`git write-tree`; detects an unresolved merge conflict in the index via the typed `*ExitError` and returns a CLEAR error per FR8 BEFORE any generation), `CommitTree(parent, msg, tree string) (sha, err)` (`git commit-tree -m <msg> <tree>`, OMITTING `-p` when parent is empty for the root commit per FR39), and `UpdateRefCAS(ref, newSHA, expected string) error` (`git update-ref <ref> <newSHA> <expected>` 2-arg CAS form that refuses if HEAD moved; the no-expected 1-arg form ONLY when expected is empty for the root commit; NEVER `--force`). All four funnel through the shipped, unexported `(g *Git) run(args...)` exec seam (P1.M3.T1.S1) against the REAL git binary (no go-git, no `sh -c`, PRD §22.3/§19). Plus internal/git/plumbing_test.go (white-box `package git`, stdlib testing ONLY, driving the REAL git binary via the S2 temp-repo harness) covering root commit, normal CAS success, CAS success when HEAD unchanged, CAS FAILURE + atomic-HEAD-unchanged when HEAD moved concurrently, and WriteTree's clear conflict error on a staged merge conflict. DOCS impact: Mode A — godoc on every exported method citing PRD §13.2/§13.5/§18.1, FR7/FR8/FR39/FR40/FR41, and decisions.md §7 (safety invariants: never git commit, never update-ref --force, never 1-arg update-ref except root commit).
context:
# PRP — P1.M3.T2.S1: internal/git/plumbing.go — RevParseHEAD, WriteTree, CommitTree, UpdateRefCAS

## Goal

**Feature Goal**: Ship the four atomicity primitives that underpin Stagehand's snapshot-based commit flow (PRD §13.2): read the current HEAD parent, freeze the index into an immutable tree object, build a dangling commit object from that tree, and CAS-advance HEAD — each a thin method over the shipped `g.run` exec seam against the REAL git binary. These are the literal primitives the generate orchestrator (P1.M6.T1.S1) calls at its commit step and the §18.1 safety invariant rests on.

**Deliverable**: TWO NEW files in the EXISTING `package git` (do NOT create a new package — plumbing.go uses a plain `package git` line; git.go already owns the `// Package git` doc):
- `internal/git/plumbing.go` — four exported methods on `*Git`: `RevParseHEAD`, `WriteTree`, `CommitTree`, `UpdateRefCAS`.
- `internal/git/plumbing_test.go` — white-box `package git`, stdlib `testing` ONLY, drives the REAL git binary via the S2 harness (`newTempRepo`/`seedCommits`/`writeFileStage` in `gittestutil_test.go`), one behavior per `Test*`.

**Success Definition**: `go test ./internal/git/` passes against the real git binary (git 2.54.0). `RevParseHEAD` returns `("some-sha", true, nil)` in a repo with commits and `("", false, nil)` in an unborn (rootless) repo. `WriteTree` returns a 40-hex tree SHA and, when the index holds an unresolved merge conflict, returns a CLEAR error whose message mentions "conflict" (FR8) without ever reaching generation. `CommitTree("", msg, tree)` creates a root commit (no `-p`); `CommitTree(parent, msg, tree)` creates a child commit; neither touches HEAD. `UpdateRefCAS("HEAD", new, expected)` with `expected==""` advances HEAD on an unborn repo; with the correct `expected` it succeeds when HEAD is unchanged and FAILS (returns non-nil error, HEAD byte-for-byte unchanged) when HEAD moved concurrently. `go build ./internal/git/`, `go vet ./internal/git/`, `gofmt`, and whole-module `go test ./...` all green. git.go/Makefile/go.mod untouched; no go-git, no `go mod tidy`.

## Why

- **The §18.1 invariant is encoded HERE, not in generate.** "The repository's refs and index are modified only at the final `update-ref` step, and only if HEAD is unchanged since the snapshot." `write-tree` is read-only w.r.t. refs/index; `commit-tree` is read-only w.r.t. refs; only `update-ref` CAS touches HEAD, and only atomically. Exposing these as narrow, correct methods makes the invariant structural — the generate layer cannot accidentally `git commit` or `update-ref --force` because those verbs do not exist in this package.
- **The proven `commit-pi` pipeline (reference_impl.md §1) ports verbatim here.** `PARENT_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)` → `git.RevParseHEAD`; `TREE_SHA=$(git write-tree)` → `git.WriteTree`; `git commit-tree ...` → `git.CommitTree`; `git update-ref HEAD NEW PARENT` → `git.UpdateRefCAS`. This task is the faithful Go port of the reference's steps 2 and 5.
- **Unblocks the whole downstream DAG.** generate's `CommitStaged` commit step (M6.T1.S1), the rescue message's manual recovery command (M6.T2), and the safety-invariant proof tests (M6.T3.S3: atomic HEAD, idempotent index, snapshot immutability) all call these four methods. Getting the CAS edge cases right now (root commit, HEAD-moved refusal, conflict detection) prevents a data-integrity regression in the core IP.
- **Shells out to real git, NOT go-git (PRD §22.3).** go-git re-implements plumbing we trust the real binary for. These methods are `g.run(args...)` calls with the typed `*ExitError` already doing the heavy lifting; each adds only a few lines of arg-building + stderr-sniffing.

## What (technical requirements)

These methods live on `*Git` in a NEW file `internal/git/plumbing.go` with a PLAIN `package git` line (git.go owns the package doc — do NOT duplicate it). They all call the unexported `g.run(args ...string) (string, error)` (shipped in S1), which returns RAW stdout (trailing newline INCLUDED) on success, a typed `*ExitError{Args, Code, Stderr}` on a non-zero git exit, or a wrapped non-typed error on a start failure. Every method therefore `strings.TrimSpace`s success stdout and uses `errors.As(err, &ee)` with `ee *ExitError` to inspect `ee.Stderr` on failure.

1. **`RevParseHEAD() (sha string, hasParent bool, err error)`** — VERIFIED behavior (host git 2.54.0):
   - Call `out, runErr := g.run("rev-parse", "HEAD")`.
   - On a NON-UNBORN error → `errors.As(runErr, &ee)`; if the repo is UNBORN (no commits yet) git exits **128** with stderr `fatal: ambiguous argument 'HEAD': unknown revision or path not in the working tree.` Detect this rootless case by `ee.Code == 128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "unknown revision")` and return `("", false, nil)` — a NIL error, `hasParent=false` (contract: "treat non-zero/empty as ok=false, not error"). This is the v1 root-commit path.
   - A DIFFERENT non-zero error (e.g. `not a git repository` — a genuine failure, not an unborn HEAD) is returned as-is (the typed `*ExitError`) — do NOT swallow it as `hasParent=false`.
   - On success: `sha = strings.TrimSpace(out)`. If `sha == ""` (defensive; shouldn't happen for `rev-parse HEAD`) return `("", false, nil)`; else return `(sha, true, nil)`.
   - godoc cites PRD §13.5 ("PARENT_SHA is captured before write-tree") and that empty/non-resolving HEAD means "root commit / first commit" (FR39), so callers omit `-p`.

2. **`WriteTree() (string, error)`** — VERIFIED behavior:
   - Call `out, err := g.run("write-tree")`.
   - On `err != nil`: `errors.As(err, &ee)` into `*ExitError`. An unresolved MERGE CONFLICT in the index makes git exit **128** with per-file `<path>: unmerged (<sha>)` lines plus `fatal: git-write-tree: error building trees` in `ee.Stderr`. ⚠️ **FR8's wording is "unresolved merge conflicts" but git 2.54.0 NEVER prints that literal phrase — the reliable signal is the substring `"unmerged"`.** If `strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "unmerged")`, return a CLEAR wrapped error, e.g. `fmt.Errorf("git: unresolved merge conflicts in index (resolve them before generating): %s", strings.TrimSpace(ee.Stderr))` (surface the git detail; the word "conflict" satisfies FR8 clarity). ANY other non-zero exit → return the typed error as-is (it is already a clear `*ExitError`).
   - On success: return `(strings.TrimSpace(out), nil)`. write-tree is read-only w.r.t. HEAD and the index (PRD §13.2 step 1) — verified: empty index yields the well-known empty-tree SHA `4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904`, and a later `git add` does NOT change a previously returned TREE_SHA (snapshot immutability).
   - godoc cites PRD §13.2 step 1, FR7 (snapshot the index), FR8 (abort on conflict before generation), and decisions.md §7.

3. **`CommitTree(parent, msg, tree string) (string, error)`** — VERIFIED behavior:
   - Build args CONDITIONALLY on `parent != ""`:
     - `parent == ""` (ROOT commit, FR39): `args := []string{"commit-tree", "-m", msg, tree}` (OMIT `-p`).
     - `parent != ""`: `args := []string{"commit-tree", "-p", parent, "-m", msg, tree}`.
   - Call `out, err := g.run(args...)`. On `err != nil` return `(strings.TrimSpace(""), err)`-style: return `("", err)` (the typed error). On success return `(strings.TrimSpace(out), nil)`.
   - `msg` is passed as ONE `-m` arg (a single `-m` containing newlines preserves a multi-line/multi-paragraph body verbatim — verified in S2 research via `git log --format=%B`; do NOT split into multiple `-m` flags, and `g.run` has `cmd.Stdin=nil` so no `-F -`).
   - `commit-tree` does NOT touch any ref (PRD §13.2 step 2): verified — a root `commit-tree` on an unborn repo leaves `rev-parse HEAD` still exiting 128 until `update-ref` runs. So this method only writes an (initially dangling) commit object to the object store.
   - godoc cites PRD §13.2 step 2, FR39 (omit `-p` for root), and that the returned SHA is dangling until `UpdateRefCAS` advances HEAD.

4. **`UpdateRefCAS(ref, newSHA, expected string) error`** — VERIFIED behavior:
   - Build args CONDITIONALLY on `expected != ""`:
     - `expected == ""` (ROOT commit / unborn HEAD — decisions.md §7: "the 1-arg no-expected form is only legal here"): `args := []string{"update-ref", ref, newSHA}`.
     - `expected != ""` (normal CAS): `args := []string{"update-ref", ref, newSHA, expected}` (the 3-arg form `<ref> <new> <expected>`).
   - Call `_, err := g.run(args...)`. Return `err` directly: `nil` on success; the typed `*ExitError` on failure.
   - NEVER append `--force` (decisions.md §7; PRD §18.2 "do not force-update"). The CAS-failure error is NORMAL and routable: when HEAD moved concurrently, git exits **128** with stderr `fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'HEAD': cannot lock ref 'HEAD': is at <actual> but expected <expected>`. The generate layer turns this into the §18.2 "HEAD moved from <parent> to <actual>" message + exit 1; this method just surfaces git's error.
   - ⚠️ **ATOMIC INVARIANT CONFIRMED**: on a CAS failure HEAD is byte-for-byte UNCHANGED (still the pre-call actual value, NOT `newSHA`). The test MUST assert this (PRD §18.1; decisions.md §7).
   - godoc cites PRD §13.2 step 3, FR40 (2-arg CAS form refuses if HEAD moved), FR41 (abort on CAS failure, never force), the root-commit 1-arg exception, and decisions.md §7.

5. **`internal/git/plumbing_test.go`** — white-box `package git` (so it can call `g.run` and read `g.dir`; the S2 harness `newTempRepo`/`seedCommits`/`writeFileStage` are also `package git` `_test.go` helpers in the same directory, so they are reachable). stdlib `testing` ONLY. Drive the REAL git binary; one behavior per `Test*`. Required cases (see Implementation Tasks for exact construction):
   - `TestRevParseHEAD_UnbornRepo` — `newTempRepo` (unborn); `RevParseHEAD()` → `hasParent==false`, `err==nil`, `sha==""`.
   - `TestRevParseHEAD_RepoWithCommits` — `seedCommits` of ≥1; `RevParseHEAD()` → `hasParent==true`, `err==nil`, `sha` is 40 hex (cross-check `== strings.TrimSpace(g.run("rev-parse","HEAD"))`).
   - `TestWriteTree_EmptyIndex` — unborn; `WriteTree()` → the empty-tree SHA `4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904`, `err==nil`.
   - `TestWriteTree_StagedContent` — `writeFileStage`; `WriteTree()` → 40-hex, `err==nil`; assert index UNCHANGED by snapshotting `git diff --cached --name-only` before & after (idempotent index, §18.1).
   - `TestWriteTree_StagedConflict` — construct a REAL merge conflict (base commit → two branches editing the same line → `git merge` → CONFLICT, leaving `UU <path>` in the index), then `WriteTree()` returns a non-nil error whose `.Error()` contains "conflict" (FR8 clarity).
   - `TestCommitTree_RootNoParent` — unborn; `tree := WriteTree()`; `sha, err := CommitTree("", "root msg", tree)` → `err==nil`, 40-hex; assert HEAD STILL unborn afterward (`RevParseHEAD().hasParent==false`) — commit-tree touches no ref.
   - `TestCommitTree_WithParent` — `seedCommits` of ≥1; capture parent via `RevParseHEAD`; stage new content → `tree := WriteTree()`; `newSHA, err := CommitTree(parent, "child msg", tree)` → `err==nil`, 40-hex, `newSHA != parent`.
   - `TestUpdateRefCAS_RootCommit` — unborn; `tree := WriteTree()`; `root := CommitTree("", "root", tree)`; `UpdateRefCAS("HEAD", root, "")` → `err==nil`; then `RevParseHEAD()` → `hasParent==true`, `sha==root` (HEAD advanced by the 1-arg form).
   - `TestUpdateRefCAS_SucceedsWhenHEADUnchanged` — seeded repo; parent, tree, newSHA as above; `UpdateRefCAS("HEAD", newSHA, parent)` → `err==nil`; `RevParseHEAD().sha == newSHA`.
   - `TestUpdateRefCAS_FailsWhenHEADMoved` — seeded repo; capture parent; move HEAD concurrently (e.g. another `seedCommits`/`g.run("commit",...)` or `CommitTree`+`UpdateRefCAS`); then call `UpdateRefCAS("HEAD", newSHA, parent)` with the STALE parent → returns a NON-NIL error; AND assert `RevParseHEAD().sha` equals the post-move HEAD, NOT `newSHA` (atomic HEAD invariant, §18.1). Also assert the error is a `*ExitError` (errors.As) with `Code==128`.

### Success Criteria
- [ ] `internal/git/plumbing.go` exists in the EXISTING `package git` with a PLAIN `package git` line (no second `// Package git` doc) and imports ONLY stdlib already used by git.go (`errors`, `fmt`, `strings`) — no go-git, no new deps, no `go mod tidy`.
- [ ] `RevParseHEAD() (string, bool, error)` returns `(40-hex, true, nil)` with commits; `("", false, nil)` on an unborn repo (NIL error); and surfaces a genuine error (e.g. not-a-repo) as-is.
- [ ] `WriteTree() (string, error)` returns a 40-hex tree SHA; on an unresolved merge conflict returns a CLEAR error whose message contains "conflict" (matching `unmerged` in git's stderr), never reaching generation.
- [ ] `CommitTree(parent, msg, tree string) (string, error)` OMITS `-p` when `parent==""` (root, FR39), includes `-p <parent>` otherwise; passes `msg` as one `-m` arg; returns a 40-hex commit SHA; touches no ref.
- [ ] `UpdateRefCAS(ref, newSHA, expected string) error` uses the 3-arg CAS form when `expected != ""`, the 1-arg no-expected form ONLY when `expected == ""` (root), NEVER `--force`; returns nil on success and a typed `*ExitError` on CAS failure; HEAD is byte-for-byte unchanged after a failure.
- [ ] `internal/git/plumbing_test.go` (white-box `package git`, stdlib testing, REAL git binary via S2 harness) covers all 10 scenarios above and PASSES.
- [ ] `go build ./internal/git/`, `go vet ./internal/git/`, and `gofmt -l internal/git/` clean; `go test ./internal/git/` and `go test ./...` exit 0.
- [ ] Mode A docs: every exported method has godoc citing PRD §13.2/§13.5/§18.1, FR7/FR8/FR39/FR40/FR41, and decisions.md §7. No README/docs created; git.go/git_test.go/gittestutil_test.go/main.go/Makefile/go.mod/go.sum/internal/ui/internal/provider unchanged.

## All Needed Context

### Documentation & References
```yaml
- file: PRD.md  # §13.2 (lines 784-792) — the three plumbing commands & their read-only w.r.t. refs semantics.
  why: "write-tree freezes the index (no ref/index mutation); commit-tree creates a dangling commit (no ref touch); update-ref HEAD <new> <expected> is the 2-arg CAS that atomically advances HEAD only if unchanged."
  critical: write-tree & commit-tree MUST NOT touch HEAD/index — only update-ref does, atomically.
- file: PRD.md  # §13.5 (lines 820-824) — edge cases: rootless repo (no -p, no expected-old), unresolved merge conflict (write-tree fails → abort), HEAD moved (CAS fails → no force).
  why: "Maps each edge case to its handling — this is the exact branching logic of RevParseHEAD/WriteTree/CommitTree/UpdateRefCAS."
  critical: FR8 wording 'unresolved merge conflicts' is NOT git's literal stderr; git says 'unmerged' — detect that.
- file: PRD.md  # §18.1 (line 1156) + §18.2 table (lines 1158-1170) — the atomicity invariant + failure modes.
  why: "Atomic HEAD after CAS failure; merge-conflict-in-index → write-tree abort; CAS failure → do NOT force."
- file: PRD.md  # FR7/FR8/FR39/FR40/FR41 (lines 242-297) — the functional requirements these methods satisfy.
- file: plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/decisions.md  # §7 (safety invariants) + §3 (generate commit step pseudocode).
  why: "NEVER git commit; NEVER update-ref --force; NEVER 1-arg update-ref EXCEPT root commit. The generate COMMIT block calls exactly CommitTree + UpdateRefCAS."

  why: "The proven commit-pi mechanics this ports: parent captured BEFORE write-tree; CAS form on the non-root path."

  why: "plumbing.go calls g.run(args...) and errors.As(&ee) into *ExitError, reading ee.Stderr. run returns RAW stdout (TrimSpace for SHAs)."
  pattern: one method = build []string args → g.run → TrimSpace on success / errors.As + stderr sniff on failure.
  gotcha: read stderr from OUR *ExitError.Stderr, never (*exec.ExitError).Stderr (empty when cmd.Stderr is set — git.go already does this for us).
- file: internal/git/gittestutil_test.go  # THE S2 harness: newTempRepo(t) (unborn repo, returns *Git), seedCommits(t,g,msgs), writeFileStage(t,g,path,content).
  why: "plumbing_test.go composes these helpers; it is white-box package git so they are reachable in the same dir."

- file: plan/001_f1f80943ac34/prps/research/P1_M3_T2_S1_research.md  # THIS task's empirical verification (all git outputs/exit codes/stderr captured on host git 2.54.0).
- url: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-update-ref  # section "--create-reflog" / the 3-arg `update-ref <ref> <new> <old>` CAS semantics.
  why: "Confirms `<old>` makes update-ref refuse if the ref is not at <old> (CAS). The 2-token form (no <old>) always updates — legal ONLY for the root commit."
- url: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-commit-tree  # `-p <parent>` (repeatable) and `-m <message>`.
  why: "Confirms omitting -p creates a root commit; a single -m carries a full message body."
- url: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-write-tree  # serializes the index; fails on unmerged index entries.
```

### Known gotchas of our codebase & library quirks
```go
// CRITICAL (FR8 wording vs git reality): git write-tree NEVER prints "unresolved
// merge conflicts". On a conflicted index git 2.54.0 prints per-file
// "<path>: unmerged (<sha>)" lines + "fatal: git-write-tree: error building trees"
// and exits 128. Detect the conflict via the substring "unmerged" on the *ExitError.Stderr.
//
// CRITICAL (unborn HEAD detection): `git rev-parse HEAD` on a repo with no commits
// exits 128 with stderr "fatal: ambiguous argument 'HEAD': unknown revision...". This
// is NOT an error for Stagehand — it is the root-commit case (hasParent=false, err=nil).
// Distinguish it from a real failure (e.g. "not a git repository") by checking the
// stderr contains "unknown revision" (stable, semantically precise).
//
// CRITICAL (run() returns RAW stdout): SHAs come back with a trailing "\n"; every
// method MUST strings.TrimSpace success output before returning a SHA.
//
// CRITICAL (read OUR buffer): on a non-zero exit run() returns *git.ExitError whose
// Stderr is OUR buffer — do NOT touch (*exec.ExitError).Stderr (empty). errors.As into
// *git.ExitError (the unqualified package type).
//
// CRITICAL (CAS atomicity): on update-ref CAS failure HEAD is byte-for-byte unchanged.
// The atomic-HEAD test asserts RevParseHEAD() == the post-move value, NOT newSHA.
//
// GOTCHA (1-arg update-ref): the no-expected form (`update-ref HEAD <new>`) ALWAYS
// updates unconditionally — it is ONLY safe for the root commit (expected==""). On any
// repo with an existing HEAD you MUST pass expected (the 3-arg CAS form) or you lose
// the atomic guarantee (decisions.md §7).
//
// GOTCHA (msg with newlines): pass the ENTIRE message as ONE -m arg; multiple -m flags
// split it into paragraphs. g.run has cmd.Stdin=nil so -F - is unusable anyway.
```

## Implementation Blueprint

### Data models / structure
```go
// plumbing.go — NO new types. These are methods on the shipped *Git; they reuse
// the shipped *ExitError via errors.As. Only stdlib imports needed: errors, fmt, strings.
package git

import (
    "errors"
    "fmt"
    "strings"
)

func (g *Git) RevParseHEAD() (sha string, hasParent bool, err error) { /* §1 */ }
func (g *Git) WriteTree() (string, error)                            { /* §2 */ }
func (g *Git) CommitTree(parent, msg, tree string) (string, error)   { /* §3 */ }
func (g *Git) UpdateRefCAS(ref, newSHA, expected string) error       { /* §4 */ }
```

### Implementation Tasks (ordered by dependencies)
```yaml
Task 1: CREATE internal/git/plumbing.go
  - NEW file; PLAIN `package git` line (git.go OWNS the package doc — do NOT add a second one).
  - IMPORTS: stdlib ONLY — errors, fmt, strings. (No bytes/os/exec needed — g.run encapsulates exec.)
  - RevParseHEAD: g.run("rev-parse","HEAD"); on runErr, errors.As(&ee) into *ExitError; if ee.Code==128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr,"unknown revision") → return ("",false,nil); else return ("",false,runErr). On nil err: sha=strings.TrimSpace(out); if sha=="" → ("",false,nil) else (sha,true,nil). godoc cites PRD §13.5/FR39 + the root-commit semantics.
  - WriteTree: out,err := g.run("write-tree"); on err → errors.As(&ee); if strings.Contains(ee.Stderr,"unmerged") → return "", fmt.Errorf("git: unresolved merge conflicts in index (resolve them before generating): %s", strings.TrimSpace(ee.Stderr)); else return "",err. On nil err → (strings.TrimSpace(out),nil). godoc cites PRD §13.2/FR7/FR8 + decisions.md §7.
  - CommitTree: args := []string{"commit-tree","-m",msg,tree}; if parent != "" { args = append([]string{"commit-tree","-p",parent,"-m",msg,tree}) } (build the full slice, do NOT append to avoid flag ordering issues — construct explicitly). out,err := g.run(args...); on err return "",err; else return strings.TrimSpace(out),nil. godoc cites PRD §13.2/FR39 + that the result is dangling until UpdateRefCAS.
  - UpdateRefCAS: args := []string{"update-ref",ref,newSHA}; if expected != "" { args = append(args, expected) } (root: 1-arg form; else 3-arg CAS). _,err := g.run(args...); return err. NEVER append "--force". godoc cites PRD §13.2/FR40/FR41 + decisions.md §7 (1-arg only for root; never force).
  - NAMING/FOLLOW: exported methods on *Git; doc density like git.go; one behavior per method.

Task 2: CREATE internal/git/plumbing_test.go
  - `package git` (white-box — uses g.run directly + reads g.dir; composes the S2 helpers newTempRepo/seedCommits/writeFileStage which are package-git _test.go in the SAME dir). stdlib testing ONLY. Import: errors, strings, testing (+ fmt if needed).
  - TestRevParseHEAD_UnbornRepo: g:=newTempRepo(t); sha,has,err:=g.RevParseHEAD(); has==false; err==nil; sha=="".
  - TestRevParseHEAD_RepoWithCommits: g:=newTempRepo(t); seedCommits(t,g,[]string{"a","b"}); sha,has,err:=g.RevParseHEAD(); err==nil; has==true; len(sha)==40; cross-check sha==strings.TrimSpace of g.run("rev-parse","HEAD").
  - TestWriteTree_EmptyIndex: g:=newTempRepo(t); tree,err:=g.WriteTree(); err==nil; tree=="4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904".
  - TestWriteTree_StagedContent: g:=newTempRepo(t); seedCommits(t,g,[]string{"a"}); writeFileStage(t,g,"new.txt","x\n"); tree,err:=g.WriteTree(); err==nil; len(tree)==40; assert index unchanged: before,_:=g.run("diff","--cached","--name-only"); _=tree; after,_:=g.run("diff","--cached","--name-only"); before==after (idempotent index, §18.1).
  - TestWriteTree_StagedConflict: g:=newTempRepo(t); seedCommits(t,g,[]string{"base"}); create a second branch that edits the same line of a tracked file; run a porcelain `git merge` that hits a CONFLICT (exit 1 is fine — use mustRun-tolerant: capture the non-zero merge via a raw g.run and ignore its error, OR drive the merge via exec inline). After the conflict the index holds `UU <path>`. _,err:=g.WriteTree(); err!=nil; strings.Contains(err.Error(),"conflict").
  - TestCommitTree_RootNoParent: g:=newTempRepo(t); writeFileStage(t,g,"a.txt","a\n"); tree,err:=g.WriteTree(); root,err:=g.CommitTree("","root msg",tree); err==nil; len(root)==40; assert HEAD STILL unborn: _,has,_:=g.RevParseHEAD(); has==false (commit-tree touches no ref).
  - TestCommitTree_WithParent: g:=newTempRepo(t); seedCommits(t,g,[]string{"first"}); parent,_,_:=g.RevParseHEAD(); writeFileStage(t,g,"b.txt","b\n"); tree,_:=g.WriteTree(); child,err:=g.CommitTree(parent,"second",tree); err==nil; len(child)==40; child!=parent.
  - TestUpdateRefCAS_RootCommit: g:=newTempRepo(t); writeFileStage(t,g,"a.txt","a\n"); tree,_:=g.WriteTree(); root,_:=g.CommitTree("","root",tree); err:=g.UpdateRefCAS("HEAD",root,""); err==nil; sha,has,_:=g.RevParseHEAD(); has==true; sha==root.
  - TestUpdateRefCAS_SucceedsWhenHEADUnchanged: g:=newTempRepo(t); seedCommits(t,g,[]string{"first"}); parent,_,_:=g.RevParseHEAD(); writeFileStage(t,g,"b.txt","b\n"); tree,_:=g.WriteTree(); newSHA,_:=g.CommitTree(parent,"second",tree); err:=g.UpdateRefCAS("HEAD",newSHA,parent); err==nil; sha,_,_:=g.RevParseHEAD(); sha==newSHA.
  - TestUpdateRefCAS_FailsWhenHEADMoved: g:=newTempRepo(t); seedCommits(t,g,[]string{"first"}); parent,_,_:=g.RevParseHEAD(); writeFileStage(t,g,"b.txt","b\n"); tree,_:=g.WriteTree(); newSHA,_:=g.CommitTree(parent,"candidate",tree); // move HEAD concurrently: seedCommits(t,g,[]string{"second"}) advances HEAD via porcelain commit; err:=g.UpdateRefCAS("HEAD",newSHA,parent); err!=nil; movedHEAD,_,_:=g.RevParseHEAD(); movedHEAD!=parent; movedHEAD!=newSHA (atomic: HEAD unchanged by the failed CAS = the post-move value, NOT newSHA). Optionally assert errors.As(&ee) && ee.Code==128.
  - NAMING: Test{Method}_{Scenario}; one behavior per function; t.Helper via the harness.
```

### Implementation Patterns & Key Details
```go
// The shared shape of every plumbing method (build []string → run → trim/errors.As):
func (g *Git) WriteTree() (string, error) {
    out, err := g.run("write-tree")
    if err != nil {
        var ee *ExitError                 // unqualified package type — our ExitError
        if errors.As(err, &ee) && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "unmerged") {
            return "", fmt.Errorf("git: unresolved merge conflicts in index (resolve them before generating): %s", strings.TrimSpace(ee.Stderr))
        }
        return "", err                    // already a clear *ExitError for any other non-zero exit
    }
    return strings.TrimSpace(out), nil    // run returns RAW stdout incl. trailing "\n"
}

// CommitTree builds args explicitly per branch (do not append -p later — flag order matters):
func (g *Git) CommitTree(parent, msg, tree string) (string, error) {
    var args []string
    if parent != "" {
        args = []string{"commit-tree", "-p", parent, "-m", msg, tree}   // child commit
    } else {
        args = []string{"commit-tree", "-m", msg, tree}                  // ROOT (FR39), omit -p
    }
    out, err := g.run(args...)
    if err != nil {
        return "", err
    }
    return strings.TrimSpace(out), nil
}

// UpdateRefCAS: 1-arg form ONLY for root (expected==""); 3-arg CAS otherwise; NEVER --force.
func (g *Git) UpdateRefCAS(ref, newSHA, expected string) error {
    args := []string{"update-ref", ref, newSHA}
    if expected != "" {
        args = append(args, expected)     // 3-arg CAS form <ref> <new> <old>
    }
    _, err := g.run(args...)              // nil on success; *ExitError (Code 128) on CAS failure
    return err
}

// RevParseHEAD: unborn repo (no commits) is hasParent=false with a NIL error.
func (g *Git) RevParseHEAD() (sha string, hasParent bool, err error) {
    out, runErr := g.run("rev-parse", "HEAD")
    if runErr != nil {
        var ee *ExitError
        if errors.As(runErr, &ee) && ee.Code == 128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "unknown revision") {
            return "", false, nil         // unborn HEAD = root-commit case (NOT an error)
        }
        return "", false, runErr          // genuine failure (e.g. not-a-repo) — surface as-is
    }
    sha = strings.TrimSpace(out)
    if sha == "" {
        return "", false, nil
    }
    return sha, true, nil
}
```

```yaml
  - internal/generate/rescue.go (M6.T2): prints the manual recovery `git commit-tree [-p PARENT] -m "..." TREE | xargs git update-ref HEAD`.

NOT HERE: the generate orchestrator, rescue message, signal handling, config, diff, log, staging, CLI wiring.
```

## DOCS Impact
**Mode A.** Per the work item: godoc on `RevParseHEAD`, `WriteTree`, `CommitTree`, and `UpdateRefCAS` cites PRD §13.2 (plumbing semantics), §13.5 (edge cases: rootless repo / conflict / HEAD-moved), §18.1 (the atomicity invariant), FR7/FR8/FR39/FR40/FR41, and decisions.md §7 (safety invariants: never `git commit`, never `update-ref --force`, never 1-arg update-ref except root commit). Inline godoc is the ONLY documentation this task produces. No README/docs/providers created (those surfaces are fed by later milestones). This PRP surfaces the item's declared DOCS line so it is not silently dropped.

## Anti-patterns to avoid
- ❌ Do NOT add a second `// Package git` doc — git.go OWNS it; plumbing.go uses a plain `package git` line.
- ❌ Do NOT import go-git / os/exec / bytes in plumbing.go — go through the shipped `g.run` (PRD §22.3/§19). Only stdlib `errors`, `fmt`, `strings` are needed.
- ❌ Do NOT forget `strings.TrimSpace` on success stdout — `run` returns the RAW SHA WITH a trailing "\n".
- ❌ Do NOT detect a conflict via the literal "unresolved merge conflicts" — git 2.54.0 says "unmerged" + "error building trees"; match "unmerged".
- ❌ Do NOT treat an unborn-HEAD `rev-parse HEAD` (exit 128, "unknown revision") as an error — it is `hasParent=false, err=nil` (the root-commit path).
- ❌ Do NOT use `--force` on update-ref, and do NOT use the 1-arg (no-expected) form for anything except the root commit (expected=="") — decisions.md §7.
- ❌ Do NOT split a multi-line message across multiple `-m` flags — one `-m` carries the whole body.
- ❌ Do NOT modify git.go/git_test.go/gittestutil_test.go/main.go/Makefile/go.mod/go.sum, create README/docs, run `go mod tidy`, or add testify — stdlib `testing` + the S2 harness only.
implementationSteps: Create internal/git/plumbing.go as a NEW file in the EXISTING `package git` with a PLAIN `package git` line (git.go already OWNS the `// Package git` doc — do NOT add a second one). Import ONLY stdlib: `errors`, `fmt`, `strings` (no go-git, no os/exec/bytes — every git call goes through the shipped, unexported `(g *Git) run(args ...string)`; no go.mod change, no `go mod tidy`). Implement four exported methods on `*Git`, each with godoc citing PRD §13.2/§13.5/§18.1, FR7/FR8/FR39/FR40/FR41, and decisions.md §7:

• RevParseHEAD() (sha string, hasParent bool, err error): out,runErr := g.run("rev-parse","HEAD"). On runErr, errors.As(runErr,&ee) into *ExitError; if ee.Code==128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr,"unknown revision") return ("",false,nil) — the unborn/rootless repo is the root-commit case, a NIL error, NOT a failure (contract: 'treat non-zero/empty as ok=false, not error'); else return ("",false,runErr) to surface a genuine failure (e.g. not-a-repo) as-is. On nil err: sha=strings.TrimSpace(out); if sha=="" return ("",false,nil) else return (sha,true,nil).

• WriteTree() (string, error): out,err := g.run("write-tree"). On err, errors.As(err,&ee); if strings.Contains(ee.Stderr,"unmerged") return "",fmt.Errorf("git: unresolved merge conflicts in index (resolve them before generating): %s", strings.TrimSpace(ee.Stderr)) — the word 'conflict' satisfies FR8 clarity and the message aborts BEFORE any generation; else return "",err (already a clear *ExitError for any other non-zero exit). On nil err return (strings.TrimSpace(out),nil). NOTE: git 2.54.0 NEVER prints the literal FR8 phrase 'unresolved merge conflicts' — it prints '<path>: unmerged (...)' + 'fatal: git-write-tree: error building trees'; match 'unmerged'.

• CommitTree(parent, msg, tree string) (string, error): build args explicitly — if parent!="" args=[]string{"commit-tree","-p",parent,"-m",msg,tree} else args=[]string{"commit-tree","-m",msg,tree} (OMIT -p for the root commit, FR39). Pass msg as ONE -m arg (a single -m preserves a multi-line body verbatim; g.run has cmd.Stdin=nil so no -F -). out,err := g.run(args...); on err return "",err; else return strings.TrimSpace(out),nil. commit-tree touches NO ref (a root commit-tree on an unborn repo leaves rev-parse HEAD still failing until update-ref runs).

• UpdateRefCAS(ref, newSHA, expected string) error: args:=[]string{"update-ref",ref,newSHA}; if expected!="" args=append(args,expected) (the 3-arg CAS form <ref> <new> <old>); the 1-arg no-expected form is used ONLY when expected=="" (root commit — decisions.md §7). NEVER append "--force". _,err := g.run(args...); return err (nil on success; a typed *ExitError with Code 128 on a CAS failure — HEAD is byte-for-byte unchanged on failure, the §18.1 invariant).

Create internal/git/plumbing_test.go as white-box `package git` (so it can call the unexported g.run, read g.dir, and compose the S2 harness helpers newTempRepo/seedCommits/writeFileStage which are package-git _test.go files in the SAME directory). Use stdlib `testing` ONLY (import errors, strings, testing; fmt optional). Drive the REAL git binary (git 2.54.0) with one behavior per Test* function. Cases: (1) TestRevParseHEAD_UnbornRepo — newTempRepo (unborn) → RevParseHEAD() hasParent==false, err==nil, sha=="". (2) TestRevParseHEAD_RepoWithCommits — seedCommits >=1 → hasParent==true, err==nil, len(sha)==40, sha==TrimSpace(g.run("rev-parse","HEAD")). (3) TestWriteTree_EmptyIndex — unborn → WriteTree()=="4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904", err==nil. (4) TestWriteTree_StagedContent — writeFileStage → 40-hex tree, err==nil, and assert the index is UNCHANGED (snapshot git diff --cached --name-only before & after; §18.1 idempotent index).

• (5) TestWriteTree_StagedConflict — construct a REAL merge conflict: seedCommits a base commit, create a second branch editing the SAME line of a tracked file, run a porcelain `git merge` that hits CONFLICT (exit 1 is fine — drive the merge via g.run and ignore its non-zero exit, or exec inline); the index now holds `UU <path>` unmerged entries. Then WriteTree() must return a non-nil error whose .Error() contains "conflict" (FR8). (6) TestCommitTree_RootNoParent — unborn repo, tree:=WriteTree(); root:=CommitTree("","root msg",tree) → err==nil, len(root)==40, and RevParseHEAD() afterward STILL hasParent==false (commit-tree touches no ref). (7) TestCommitTree_WithParent — seedCommits >=1, parent:=RevParseHEAD().sha; stage new content → tree:=WriteTree(); child:=CommitTree(parent,"child",tree) → err==nil, len(child)==40, child!=parent.

• (8) TestUpdateRefCAS_RootCommit — unborn repo, tree:=WriteTree(), root:=CommitTree("","root",tree); UpdateRefCAS("HEAD",root,"") → err==nil; then RevParseHEAD() hasParent==true, sha==root (the 1-arg form advanced HEAD). (9) TestUpdateRefCAS_SucceedsWhenHEADUnchanged — seeded repo, parent:=RevParseHEAD().sha; stage new content, tree:=WriteTree(), newSHA:=CommitTree(parent,"second",tree); UpdateRefCAS("HEAD",newSHA,parent) → err==nil; RevParseHEAD().sha==newSHA. (10) TestUpdateRefCAS_FailsWhenHEADMoved — seeded repo, capture parent; build a candidate newSHA via CommitTree(parent,...); MOVE HEAD concurrently (seedCommits another commit, or CommitTree+UpdateRefCAS to advance HEAD past parent); then UpdateRefCAS("HEAD",newSHA,parent) with the STALE parent must return a NON-NIL error; AND assert RevParseHEAD().sha equals the post-move HEAD, NOT newSHA (atomic-HEAD invariant, §18.1). Optionally assert the error is a *ExitError with Code==128 via errors.As.

Run the validation gates IN ORDER and fix any failure before declaring done: `go build ./internal/git/` (must compile the new plumbing.go), `go vet ./internal/git/` (clean — vet COMPILES the _test.go files incl. plumbing_test.go), `test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/git/)"` (no unformatted files), `go test ./internal/git/` (PASS against the REAL git binary — all 10 scenarios + existing S1/S2 tests), `go test ./...` (whole-module integrity; Makefile `test` target green; internal/ui + internal/provider unaffected).

Create NO README.md/docs/providers TOML. successCriteria.description: internal/git/plumbing.go exists in the EXISTING package git with a PLAIN `package git` line (no second `// Package git` doc) and imports ONLY stdlib (`errors`, `fmt`, `strings`) — no go-git, no os/exec/bytes, no new go.mod dependency, no `go mod tidy`.

RevParseHEAD() (sha string, hasParent bool, err error) returns (40-hex, true, nil) in a repo with commits; returns ("", false, nil) on an unborn/rootless repo (NIL error — the root-commit case); and surfaces a genuine non-zero error (e.g. not-a-repo) as-is (typed *ExitError), distinguishing 'unknown revision' (unborn) from other failures.

WriteTree() (string, error) returns a 40-hex tree SHA on success (empty index → 4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904) and, on an unresolved merge conflict in the index, returns a CLEAR error whose message contains 'conflict' (matching git's 'unmerged' stderr substring, FR8) BEFORE any generation; other non-zero exits surface as the typed *ExitError.

CommitTree(parent, msg, tree string) (string, error) OMITS `-p` when parent=="" (root commit, FR39) and includes `-p <parent>` otherwise; passes msg as ONE `-m` arg; returns a 40-hex commit SHA; and does NOT touch any ref (verified: HEAD unchanged after a root commit-tree).

UpdateRefCAS(ref, newSHA, expected string) error uses the 3-arg CAS form `update-ref <ref> <new> <expected>` when expected!="" and the 1-arg no-expected form ONLY when expected=="" (root commit); NEVER appends `--force` (decisions.md §7); returns nil on success and a typed *ExitError (Code 128) on a CAS failure; HEAD is byte-for-byte UNCHANGED after a failure (§18.1 atomic invariant).

internal/git/plumbing_test.go is white-box `package git` (calls g.run and composes the S2 harness newTempRepo/seedCommits/writeFileStage in the same dir), stdlib testing ONLY, drives the REAL git binary, and PASSES all 10 scenarios: RevParseHEAD unborn/with-commits, WriteTree empty-index/staged-content/index-unchanged/staged-conflict, CommitTree root-no-parent/with-parent, UpdateRefCAS root-commit/succeeds-when-unchanged/fails-when-moved-and-head-unchanged.

go build ./internal/git/ and go vet ./internal/git/ are clean; `test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/git/)"` produces no output; `go test ./internal/git/` and `go test ./...` both exit 0 (whole-module integrity; internal/ui + internal/provider tests unaffected).

No README/docs/providers created; git.go, git_test.go, gittestutil_test.go, main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, internal/ui, internal/provider unchanged; the `// Package git` doc appears exactly once (in git.go).

references: PRD.md §13.2 (lines 784-792) — the three plumbing commands: write-tree freezes the index (no ref/index mutation), commit-tree creates a dangling commit (no ref touch), update-ref HEAD <new> <expected> is the 2-arg CAS that advances HEAD only if unchanged. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md §13.5 (lines 820-824) — edge cases: rootless repo (commit-tree no -p, update-ref no expected-old), unresolved merge conflict (write-tree fails → abort), HEAD moved during generation (CAS fails → no force, print manual recovery). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md
PRD.md §18.1 (line 1156) — the atomicity invariant: refs/index modified only at the final update-ref step, only if HEAD unchanged since snapshot. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md §18.2 (lines 1158-1170) — failure-mode table: merge-conflict-in-index → write-tree abort (exit 1); update-ref CAS failure → print message + manual recovery, do NOT force (exit 1). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md FR7/FR8/FR39/FR40/FR41 (lines 242-297) — FR7 snapshot index with write-tree; FR8 abort on write-tree conflict before generation; FR39 commit-tree omit -p for root; FR40 update-ref 2-arg CAS form; FR41 abort on CAS failure, never force. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

git-update-ref documentation — the 3-arg `update-ref <ref> <new> <old>` CAS form refuses if the ref is not at <old>; the 2-token (no <old>) form always updates (legal ONLY for the root commit). https://git-scm.com/docs/git-update-ref
git-commit-tree documentation — `-p <parent>` (repeatable; omit for a root commit) and `-m <message>` (a single -m carries a full multi-line body). https://git-scm.com/docs/git-commit-tree
git-write-tree documentation — serializes the current index into a tree object; read-only w.r.t. refs; fails on an index with unmerged entries. https://git-scm.com/docs/git-write-tree
